recent_decisions.title: Scrutiny Committee notes Pay Policy Statement 2026/27
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee noted the Pay Policy Statement, which includes pay ratios of 5.2 (lowest to CEO) and 3.3 (lowest to Chief Officer). The gender pay gap reporting continues despite a workforce under 250.

Pay ratio of lowest to Chief Officer: 3.3
Gender pay gap reporting continues despite workforce under 250

recent_decisions.title: Approval to demolish former Adelphi Hotel under Building Act 1984
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council approved demolishing the former Adelphi Hotel to enforce a Magistrates Court order under the Building Act 1984. The Head of Housing and Development Control was delegated authority to recover the debt.
key_data_points: Demolition under Section 77 of the Building Act 1984
Magistrates Court Order enforced
Debt recovery authority delegated to Head of Housing

motions.text: The carrying out of work in default to demolish the former Adelphi Hotel to enforce the Magistrates Court Order secured under section 77 of The Building Act 1984.

That authority is delegated to the Head of Housing and Development Control to negotiate how best to recover the debt that will be owed to the Council, including but not limited to the options set out in paragraph 13.

recent_decisions.title: Approved minutes of previous meetings
recent_decisions.political_summary: The minutes of the November and December 2025 meetings were approved without discussion.
key_data_points: Minutes approved as correct record

motions.text: The latest revised net budget of £18.721m as shown in Table 1, and the net transfers to date from earmarked reserves of £5.082m, further details of which can be seen in Appendix 2.

recent_decisions.title: Referred motion on fly-tipping funding
recent_decisions.political_summary: A motion to allocate savings to environmental services was referred to the Executive without discussion due to expenditure rules.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Allocation of savings to environmental services
key_data_points: Motion referred to Executive
Focus on fly-tipping and street cleansing

motions.text: If the Government postpones the elections and is successful in the Judicial Review litigation, this Council agrees to allocate 100% of the savings specifically to boost local environmental services—particularly those dedicated to tackling fly-tipping, street cleansing and environmental enforcement.

recent_decisions.title: Noted revenue budget forecast
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council noted a projected net overspend of £128k, down from £155k in Q1, expected to break even by year-end.
key_data_points: Net overspend £128k
Decrease of £27k from Q1
Expected to break even by year-end

motions.text: The quarterly update on the Treasury Management Strategy 2025/26 in compliance with the requirements of the Chartered Institute of Public Finance and Accountancy’s (CIPFA) Code of Practice on Treasury Management.

recent_decisions.title: Approved capital budget increase
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved a £105k increase in capital budget, bringing total to £24.121m, and approved financing for the revised budget.
key_data_points: Capital budget increase £105k
Revised total £24.121m
Financing approved

motions.text: That in line with those recommendations, the Members Allowance Scheme for 2026/27 should be maintained at the same level as 2025/26.

recent_decisions.title: Endorsed treasury management strategy
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council endorsed the treasury management strategy update and approved revised strategy with increased capital financing requirement.
key_data_points: Treasury management strategy endorsed
Increased capital financing requirement

motions.text: That Full Council be requested to: Endorse the approach that has been adopted in developing budget proposals that reflect the Council’s Strategic Objectives for 2026/27; Approve the proposals contained in this report with the addition of the following initiatives as set out by the Labour Group in the amendment they had proposed, together with an additional increase to the Empty Homes budget above that proposed by the Labour Group: To provide a boost to the Empty Homes Programme and to increase t

recent_decisions.title: Approved fees and charges 2026/27 with amendments
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council approved a 2% increase in fees and charges from April 2026, with amendments to exclude increases for commercial waste and bereavement services, reducing financial impact from £86k to £30k.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Reduced financial impact from amendments to fees and charges
key_data_points: 2% increase in fees and charges
Amendments to exclude commercial waste and bereavement service increases
Financial impact reduced from £86k to £30k

motions.text: Full Council endorses the Executive’s approved tariff of fees and charges from 1 April 2026 subject to the Executive reconsidering the Commercial Waste and Bereavement Service charges. Specifically, as follows: 1. Amend para 16 – Commercial Waste (2nd paragraph, 1st sentence) to read as follows: There will be no increase to the ‘Recycling Annual Contract - Weekly Collections’ annual fee. Remove the last paragraph. 2. Add a para 21a - Bereavement Services. There will be no increase to bereavement
The recommendations in the report were moved by Cllr M Lishman and seconded by Cllr Anwar.

key_data_points: 3G pitch for community use in Padiham/west Burnley

motions.text: Delegated authority to negotiate and agree lease terms for a 3G pitch at Burnley High School.

recent_decisions.title: Adjournment of Hackney Carriage Vehicle Licence Renewal Hearing
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee adjourned the hearing for a Hackney Carriage vehicle licence renewal to 18th February 2026 due to the applicant's lack of representation. No vote was recorded.
key_data_points: Hearing adjourned to 18th February 2026
Applicant requested adjournment due to lack of representation
No vote recorded on the decision

motions.text: The sub-committee considered the request at 11:00am. The sub-committee RESOLVED that the hearing be adjourned to 10:45am on Wednesday 18th February 2026.
